_meta.description: Contextual event annotations and external benchmarks for Ground Truth Strata visualizations
_meta.purpose: Event annotations appear as reference lines on time series charts. External benchmarks provide contextual comparison points. Survey wave dates establish the time axis.
_meta.note_on_benchmarks: External benchmark values are for contextual reference only. The primary national average used in Strata is computed from the full survey sample, not from these external sources. Instrument comparability varies — see comparability_note on each benchmark.

event_annotations.label: Musk acquires Twitter
event_annotations.description: Elon Musk completes $44 billion acquisition of Twitter, immediately fires top executives
event_annotations.relevance: Context for Wave 1 data — survey began ~4 months after acquisition

event_annotations.label: Twitter rebrands to X
event_annotations.description: Twitter officially rebranded to X, bird logo replaced with X logo
event_annotations.relevance: Falls between Wave 1 and Wave 2 data collection

event_annotations.label: Threads launches
event_annotations.description: Meta launches Threads, a Twitter/X competitor, reaching 100 million users in first week
event_annotations.relevance: Threads added to survey platform list in Wave 2 (UAS515); Wave 1 predates launch

event_annotations.label: Surgeon General declares loneliness epidemic
event_annotations.description: U.S. Surgeon General Vivek Murthy releases advisory 'Our Epidemic of Loneliness and Isolation,' calling loneliness a public health crisis with health risks comparable to smoking 15 cigarettes a day
event_annotations.relevance: Falls during Wave 1 data collection; directly relevant to loneliness variables in survey
event_annotations.source: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

event_annotations.label: Threads launches in EU
event_annotations.description: Threads finally launches in the European Union after regulatory delay

event_annotations.label: Biden signs TikTok divestiture law
event_annotations.description: President Biden signs law requiring ByteDance to divest TikTok's U.S. business by January 19, 2025 or face a ban
event_annotations.relevance: Falls between Wave 4 and Wave 5; likely affects Wave 5 TikTok usage and attitudes data

event_annotations.label: Paris Olympics begin
event_annotations.description: 2024 Summer Olympics open in Paris; widely credited as a moment of global positive sentiment and social media positivity
event_annotations.relevance: Overlaps with Wave 5 data collection (Jul–Sep 2024); may influence wellbeing and social media sentiment variables

event_annotations.label: U.S. Presidential Election
event_annotations.description: Donald Trump defeats Kamala Harris in U.S. presidential election
event_annotations.relevance: Falls between Wave 5 and Wave 6; Wave 6 includes vote_2024 preference variable; may affect political attitudes and wellbeing

event_annotations.label: TikTok briefly goes dark in U.S.
event_annotations.description: TikTok goes offline for approximately 12 hours in the U.S. as divestiture deadline passes without a sale; restored after Trump signals 90-day extension
event_annotations.relevance: Falls at the start of Wave 6 data collection (Jan–Mar 2025); directly relevant to TikTok usage data in Wave 6

event_annotations.label: Trump inaugurated (second term)
event_annotations.description: Donald Trump inaugurated for second presidential term; announces TikTok 90-day extension same day
event_annotations.relevance: Falls at the start of Wave 6; relevant to political attitudes and tech regulation variables

event_annotations.label: Meta ends third-party fact-checking
event_annotations.description: Mark Zuckerberg announces Meta will end third-party fact-checking programs on Facebook and Instagram, replace with community notes system similar to X
event_annotations.relevance: Falls just before Wave 6; relevant to 'bad for the world' experience variables and tech regulation attitudes

event_annotations.label: Bluesky surges to 20M users
event_annotations.description: Bluesky reaches 20 million users in weeks following 2024 U.S. election, as users migrate from X; added to survey platform list in Wave 6
event_annotations.relevance: Wave 6 is the first wave to include Bluesky as a survey option

event_annotations.label: Surgeon General warns on social media & youth
event_annotations.description: Surgeon General Murthy issues advisory warning that social media poses a 'profound risk of harm' to children and adolescents' mental health
event_annotations.relevance: Falls during Wave 1–2 gap; relevant to social media attitudes and wellbeing variables

event_annotations.label: U.S. COVID-19 emergency ends
event_annotations.description: The Biden administration ends the COVID-19 public health emergency, formally concluding the national emergency declaration first issued in March 2020
event_annotations.relevance: Falls during Wave 1; marks formal end of pandemic-era social context that shaped loneliness and social media use patterns

event_annotations.label: GPT-4 released
event_annotations.description: OpenAI releases GPT-4, marking mainstream breakthrough of generative AI; ChatGPT reaches 100M users by this point
event_annotations.relevance: Context for AI attitudes and usage variables in survey; AI questions were most extensive in Wave 1 and 2

event_annotations.label: Taylor Swift Eras Tour begins
event_annotations.description: Taylor Swift begins the Eras Tour, which becomes the highest-grossing concert tour in history and a dominant cultural and social media phenomenon throughout 2023–2024
event_annotations.relevance: Runs throughout Wave 1–5; associated with significant positive social media sentiment and community formation, relevant to meaningful connection variables

event_annotations.label: Hamas attacks Israel; Israel-Gaza war begins
event_annotations.description: Hamas launches large-scale attack on Israel; Israel begins military campaign in Gaza. Generates massive polarizing content on social media throughout Wave 3 and beyond
event_annotations.relevance: Falls during Wave 3 data collection; likely affects 'bad for the world' experience variables, political content exposure, and wellbeing

event_annotations.label: Reddit API protests / blackout
event_annotations.description: Thousands of Reddit communities go dark in protest of Reddit's API pricing changes; largest platform protest in social media history
event_annotations.relevance: Falls between Wave 1 and Wave 2; may affect Reddit usage and sentiment data in Wave 2

external_benchmarks.source: Gallup National Health and Well-Being Index
external_benchmarks.comparability_note: Gallup measures 'felt lonely a lot of the day yesterday'; survey uses UCLA-3 (Hardly ever / Some of the time / Often). Different instruments — treat as approximate context only.
external_benchmarks.use_note: Do not display as benchmark; reference in annotation text only if relevant

external_benchmarks.comparability_note: Cigna uses UCLA-20 item scale, different threshold. Survey uses UCLA-3. Not directly comparable.
external_benchmarks.use_note: Do not display as benchmark; context only

category_definitions.platform_change: Changes to platform features, ownership, policy, or availability
category_definitions.platform_launch: Launch of a new platform or major new feature
category_definitions.policy_regulation: Government legislation or regulatory actions affecting platforms
category_definitions.policy_health: Public health advisories or policy statements relevant to wellbeing/social media
category_definitions.political: Major U.S. or global political events
category_definitions.public_health: Public health events affecting the general population
category_definitions.pop_culture: Cultural moments with significant social media relevance
category_definitions.world_events: Major geopolitical or world events with social media/wellbeing relevance
